paper_map.title: Fatigue properties of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel: Critical impact of κ-carbide precipitation state
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study systematically investigates the tension-tension fatigue behavior and deformation mechanisms of solution-treated and aged Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C (wt%) austenitic low-density steels, focusing on the critical role of κ-carbide precipitation state in controlling fatigue properties. In aged samples, intragranular κ-carbides induce planar slip and enhance slip reversibility, improving fatigue life at low stress amplitudes, while intergranular κ-carbides impede slip transmission and accelerate damage, reducing fatigue life at high stress amplitudes. The findings highlight that accelerating intragranular κ-carbide precipitation while suppressing intergranular precipitation can concurrently enhance fatigue performance across all stress amplitudes.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the effect of intragranular and intergranular κ-carbide precipitation states on the fatigue behavior and deformation mechanisms of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel, and to provide microstructural design strategies for improved fatigue resistance.
main_process_variables: Hot rolling to 4 mm thickness before heat treatment
Tensile test: room temperature, strain rate 10^{-3} s^{-1}
Fatigue test: stress-controlled tension-tension, R=0.1, frequency 20 Hz, sinusoidal waveform, ambient air
Specimen: flat dog-bone shape, loading axis parallel to rolling direction

sample_aliases.description: Solution treated at 1050°C for 1h, water quenched

key_sections.purpose: Background on low-density steels, κ-carbide precipitation, and fatigue; identifies research gap and objectives.
Describes material composition, processing (ST and aging), mechanical testing (tensile and fatigue), and microstructural characterization (EBSD, TEM).
Presents undeformed microstructures, tensile properties, fatigue S-N data, fracture characteristics, crack propagation, surface deformation, and deformation microstructures.
Discusses impact of intragranular κ-carbides on dislocation slip mode, their effect on fatigue at low stress amplitudes, and effect of intergranular κ-carbides on fatigue at high stress amplitudes.
Summarizes the critical role of κ-carbide precipitation state in fatigue properties and provides microstructural design guidelines.
